title: Houses, SUVs, Rs 5-6L stolen daily: How lifestyles of Ram temple theft accused changed dramatically
summary: An investigation into Ram Temple donations in Ayodhya has revealed a stark financial uplift among accused individuals, with some building homes and acquiring SUVs despite modest known incomes. Concerns were raised about the handling of precious metal donations and the lack of proper oversight.

title: India's passport ranking slips, remains outside top 100: Which nations are in top 10?
'Watching you': Rs 200 fine for littering or spitting at stations, on trains
summary: Eastern Railway has launched a strict cleanliness drive, "Eastern Railway is Watching You," imposing a Rs 200 fine for littering and spitting. Commuters can report offenders by sending photos to a dedicated WhatsApp number, 9002022780.  CCTV surveillance will also be used to catch those defacing railway premises. Passengers reporting offenders will be recognized as "Captain Clean."

title: 'Paraguay's players were absolute disgrace': South American side slammed after WC exit
summary: France reached the quarter-finals with a tense 1-0 win over France national football team, but the match was overshadowed by the behaviour of Paraguay national football team. Joe Hart called Paraguay's players "an absolute disgrace", while Micah Richards described their conduct as "embarrassing". Kylian Mbappe scored the winning penalty to set up a quarter-final clash with Morocco.

title: 'Unwell since his death': Ketan Agarwal's grandfather dies of cardiac arrest
summary: The grandfather of Ketan Agarwal, a Pune realtor whose death at Lohagad Fort is being investigated as murder, has passed away weeks after his grandson's demise. Devichand Agarwal reportedly suffered a cardiac arrest following the shock. Police are investigating the alleged murder of Ketan Agarwal, with his fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her alleged lover arrested. A Snapchat message from Goyal, expressing doubts about the wedding, has surfaced.

title: Tim Cook, Sundar Pichai, Michael Dell and other tech CEOs share July 4 greetings
summary: Tech titans like Tim Cook, Sundar Pichai, and Elon Musk joined the nation in celebrating America's 250th Independence Day. Leaders from Apple, Google, SpaceX, and others shared heartfelt messages on social media, reflecting on the country's journey and future. Michael Dell also announced a significant donation to support American children's financial futures, highlighting a blend of patriotic sentiment and forward-looking initiatives within the tech community.

title: British GP 2026: Antonelli claims pole after sprint win, Hamilton misses out
summary: Silverstone witnessed a thrilling start to the British Grand Prix weekend, with Mercedes rookie Kimi Antonelli securing pole position for Sunday's race. Despite Lewis Hamilton's strong performance, topping practice and taking Sprint pole, Antonelli fought back to win the Sprint race and then claimed his fifth pole of the season. Ferrari's Leclerc and Hamilton will start second and third, setting up an exciting Mercedes-Ferrari battle.

title: Padma Vibhushan Teejan Bai passes away at 70; tributes pour in for Pandavani legend
summary: India mourns the loss of Padma Vibhushan Teejan Bai, a legendary Pandavani artist, who passed away at 69 after a prolonged illness. Hospitalized for breathing difficulties and weakness, her condition deteriorated at AIIMS, Raipur. Bai, a pioneer of the Mahabharata storytelling tradition, was celebrated for her dedication to preserving folk art, earning numerous prestigious awards throughout her illustrious career.

title: Kim Jong Un oversees nuclear-capable missile tests from newly repaired warship
summary: North Korea's Kim Jong Un has overseen tests of nuclear-capable cruise missiles and other systems aboard a newly repaired 5,000-ton destroyer, the Kang Kon. This move signals Pyongyang's intensified efforts to bolster its naval nuclear capabilities, following the recent commissioning of another similar vessel. Kim has ordered the Kang Kon to be operational within two months, underscoring a strategic shift towards expanding its sea-based nuclear arsenal.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
summary: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: France vs Paraguay becomes one of hottest World Cup matches ever
summary: France lead Paraguay in a FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 clash played in extreme heat at Philadelphia’s Lincoln Financial Field, with temperatures near 100°F (37.8°C) and heat index reaching 105–110°F (40.6–43.3°C). The extreme conditions, under an official heat warning, have turned the match into a severe physical test, with Kylian Mbappé’s penalty separating the sides.

title: After 8,000 layoffs, Zuckerberg admits Meta misjudged AI progress
summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged internal restructuring hasn't yielded expected results, particularly in AI agent development, admitting executives 'miscalculated' timelines.  Despite significant layoffs and AI investments, progress has been slower than anticipated.  However, Zuckerberg anticipates substantial AI benefits within three to six months.  Separately, the company addressed mouse-tracking software, assuring no employee data was used for AI training and confirming future opt-in policies.

title: Why Delhi court denied bail to Umar Khalid, Sharjeel Imam again
summary: A Delhi court has rejected the bail pleas of Umar Khalid and Sharjeel Imam in the 2020 riots larger conspiracy case. The judge stated the court must adhere to a Supreme Court ruling from January 5, 2026, which had previously dismissed their bail petitions. The court found no grounds to reconsider the matter, citing the apex court's prior decision.

title: Watch: New York's Brooklyn Bridge catches fire during July 4 fireworks display
summary: During Macy's Fourth of July fireworks extravaganza, the stunning Brooklyn Bridge briefly caught fire, sending flames and dark smoke billowing up as fireworks lit up the night sky above. Eyewitnesses reported that the fire began modestly but intensified quickly, with additional explosions reportedly triggered by the blaze. The dramatic scene unfolded over approximately one minute, leaving uncertainty about any potential damage to the historic structure.

title: 'Irreplaceable loss to art & culture': PM Modi mourns demise of folk singer Teejan Bai
summary: Renowned Pandavani singer Teejan Bai has passed away, leaving an irreplaceable void in the world of art and culture. Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Chhattisgarh Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai paid glowing tributes, highlighting her immense contribution to popularizing the traditional folk art form globally. Her powerful performances, rooted in the Mahabharata, earned her prestigious national honours and brought immense pride to her state.

title: NHL rumors: Flames, Kraken eye Sabres' $3.75m rising scorer

The 24-year-old winger has a low cap hit and recent production, making him a strong trade candidate with the Calgary Flames and Seattle Kraken among the teams linked.

title: B'luru daycare horror: Whistleblower held; panel seeks live streaming at centres
summary: Following alleged toddler abuse at a Bengaluru daycare, the Karnataka Commission for Protection of Child Rights will mandate CCTV cameras with live parental access at all centers. This move aims to enhance safety and transparency. The investigation into the incident, which involved a nanny allegedly locking a child in a bathroom and another nanny accused of suspicious video recording, is ongoing.
